findings.evidence: `rg -n "stdSymplecticForm|isLagrangian_prod" code/TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic` shows `stdSymplecticForm` is defined in `StandardCompatible.lean:78`, while the only `isLagrangian_prod_*` uses are in `Lagrangian.lean:185` and `:198`; `Lagrangian.lean:6` imports `StandardCompatible` solely for those examples.
findings.fix: Keep `Lagrangian.lean` as the core file importing `AlmostComplex` plus `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.BilinearForm.Orthogonal`; move the two `stdSymplecticForm` coordinate-factor lemmas to `StandardCompatible.lean` after importing the core file, or to a small later standard-model companion file.
findings.issue: `isLagrangian_prod_top_bot` and `isLagrangian_prod_bot_top` are standard-model facts about `stdSymplecticForm`, but they live in the generic Lagrangian file and force `import TauCeti.Geometry.Symplectic.StandardCompatible` at line 6 for all users of `IsIsotropic`/`IsLagrangian`.

summary: The generic Lagrangian vocabulary is in the right area, but the standard-product examples make the core file import the standard compatible model. Split the standard-model material out so users can import the basic predicates without pulling in inner-product standard-model machinery.
